subject: Study Skills
title: Why re-reading fails (and what works)
hook: The highest-yield study upgrade you can make this week. Swipe.

points.heading: Re-reading feels productive
points.body: Re-reading builds familiarity, not memory. You recognise the page but cannot retrieve the fact when the exam asks for it.

points.heading: Active recall
points.body: Close the notes and force yourself to retrieve the answer from memory. That effort of retrieval is what builds durable, exam-ready recall.

points.heading: Spaced repetition
points.body: Revisit each fact at growing intervals, right as you start to forget it. This is where flashcards earn their place in your week.

points.heading: Make it effortless
points.body: The hard part is turning lectures into questions. Recall Engine does that step for you, so your time goes into retrieving, not formatting.

caption: If re-reading was working, you would not forget it a week later. Familiarity is not memory. Switch to active recall, space it out, and let flashcards do the heavy lifting. The only friction is making the questions, and that is exactly what Recall Engine automates from your own lectures. Save this and change how you revise this week. Start free, link in bio.
